communications.communication:
To Whom It May Concern:

Pursuant to the Washington Public Records Act, I hereby request the following records:

All current "Brady lists" and "Giglio lists" (per Brady v. Maryland/Giglio v. United States) maintained by your office, along with accompanying potential impeachment material.

The requested documents will be made available to the general public, and this request is not being made for commercial purposes.

In the event that there are fees, I would be grateful if you would inform me of the total charges in advance of fulfilling my request. I would prefer the request filled electronically, by e-mail attachment if available or CD-ROM if not.

Thank you in advance for your anticipated cooperation in this matter. I look forward to receiving your response to this request within 5 business days, as the statute requires.

Sincerely,

Rose Terse

title: FOIA Request – Law Enforcement ADA Training Materials

communications.subject: Freedom of Information Act Request: FOIA Request – Law Enforcement ADA Training Materials
communications.communication:
To Whom It May Concern:

Pursuant to the Freedom of Information Act, I hereby request the following records:

ADA.gov (U.S. Department of Justice)

Subject: FOIA Request – Law Enforcement ADA Training Materials

To: U.S. Department of Justice, ADA Information Line

Dear FOIA Officer:

Pursuant to the federal Freedom of Information Act, I request copies of all training materials, guides, reports, and correspondence provided to Arkansas law enforcement agencies regarding:

ADA compliance and effective communication with persons with disabilities, including those who are deaf or hard of hearing.

Training resources and guides specifically referenced for Arkansas law enforcement.

Any other documents, emails, or reports provided to Arkansas agencies related to law enforcement ADA training.

Please provide these records electronically if available.
Thank you,

The requested documents will be made available to the general public, and this request is not being made for commercial purposes.

In the event that there are fees, I would be grateful if you would inform me of the total charges in advance of fulfilling my request. I would prefer the request filled electronically, by e-mail attachment if available or CD-ROM if not.

Thank you in advance for your anticipated cooperation in this matter. I look forward to receiving your response to this request within 20 business days, as the statute requires.

Sincerely,

Christopher Reed

To Whom It May Concern:

Pursuant to the Freedom of Information Act, I hereby request the following records:

Arkansas Law Enforcement Training Academy (ALETA)

Subject: FOIA Request – ADA & Disability Training Records

To: Arkansas Law Enforcement Training Academy

Dear FOIA Officer:

I am requesting, under the Arkansas FOIA, copies of all training materials, curricula, reports, and related documents pertaining to:

Training for interactions with persons with disabilities, ADA compliance, effective communication, and crisis intervention.

“Just Policing” program and specialized ADA courses.
Evaluation reports, attendance records, and instructor lists for these trainings.

Any other documents, emails, or reports relating to law enforcement officer training on ADA and disability awareness.

Please advise of any fees and provide records electronically if feasible.

Thank you,

The requested documents will be made available to the general public, and this request is not being made for commercial purposes.

In the event that there are fees, I would be grateful if you would inform me of the total charges in advance of fulfilling my request. I would prefer the request filled electronically, by e-mail attachment if available or CD-ROM if not.

Thank you in advance for your anticipated cooperation in this matter. I look forward to receiving your response to this request within 20 business days, as the statute requires.

Sincerely,

Christopher Reed

title: Law Enforcement Disability & ADA Training Records

communications.subject: Arkansas Freedom of Information Act Request: Law Enforcement Disability & ADA Training Records
communications.communication:
To Whom It May Concern:

Pursuant to the Arkansas Freedom of Information Act, I hereby request the following records:

Arkansas Department of Public Safety

Subject: FOIA Request – Law Enforcement Disability & ADA Training Records

To: Arkansas Department of Public Safety

Dear FOIA Officer:

Pursuant to the Arkansas Freedom of Information Act, I am requesting access to and copies of all records, reports, training materials, curricula, and related documents regarding the training of law enforcement officers on interactions with persons with disabilities as mandated by Arkansas Code 12-9-116. This includes, but is not limited to:

Training records and reports from the Arkansas Law Enforcement Training Academy (ALETA) related to disability awareness, ADA compliance, effective communication, and crisis intervention.

Materials regarding the “Just Policing” program and any specialized “Law Enforcement and Americans with Disabilities Act (ADA)” courses.

Documents pertaining to the Blue Envelope Initiative and any other programs designed to improve interactions between drivers with autism or mental illnesses and police.

All correspondence, internal memos, or guidance documents relating to the above training and programs.

Any other documents, emails, or reports relating to law enforcement training on disability awareness and ADA obligations.

If possible, please provide these records in electronic format. If there are any fees for searching or copying these records, please inform me in advance.

Thank you for your attention to this request.

The requested documents will be made available to the general public, and this request is not being made for commercial purposes.

In the event that there are fees, I would be grateful if you would inform me of the total charges in advance of fulfilling my request. I would prefer the request filled electronically, by e-mail attachment if available or CD-ROM if not.

Thank you in advance for your anticipated cooperation in this matter. I look forward to receiving your response to this request within 3 business days, as the statute requires.

Sincerely,

Christopher Reed
